items.title: Syrians search desperately for missing prisoners | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
Crowds of people went to Syria's Saydnaya prison for the second day in a row to look for missing loved ones, after rebels freed prisoners across the country. Rescue workers ended search operations, saying they couldn't find any underground cells at the prison.

#syria #assad #Saydnaya

items.title: Syrian rebels name Mohammed al-Bashir as interim prime minister | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
Syria's rebels have named Mohammed al-Bashir as the country's new interim prime minister. He announced that he will head up a transitional government until March 1st, 2025.

Bashir had already served as the leader of a rebel-held area in northwestern Syria before the lightning offensive that saw Assad toppled after 24 years in power.

The 41-year-old is a trained engineer who also has a degree in Sharia law from the university in Idlib, a longtime rebel stronghold.

Before becoming prime minister of what the rebels, lead by Islamist group HayĘĽat Tahrir al-Sham (HTS), dubbed the "Salvation Government" in January 2024, he worked for the group's ministry responsible for development and humanitarian aid.

Journalist Ossama Muhammad joined us from Qamishli, in northeastern Syria, to discuss Bashir's appointment and the latest developments in Syria.

We also spoke to Professor Fawaz Gerges from the London School of Economics about Syria's different rebel groups and the moderate tone HTS has been striking.

00:00 Mohammed al-Bashir appointed interim prime minister
00:29 Journalist Ossama Muhammad shares more on Bashir
04:19 Fawaz Gerges discusses HTS and the path forward for Syria
#syria #hts #assad

items.title: Will India's tech industry benefit from Trump's return to power? | DW Business
items.author: DW News
items.content:
Indian high-tech companies are likely to benefit from Trump's presidency in the US. A strong US dollar, lower taxes in the USA, and a restrictive attitude from Washington towards China are the reasons for this. But a likely stricter US immigration policy could also be a hindrance.

items.title: Is Israel exploiting Syria's instability to expand its military operations? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
Israel has launched a string of attacks on Syrian military bases, saying the strikes are needed to prevent weapons from falling into hostile hands. 

Israeli troops have also taken control of a demilitarized buffer zone separating the Israeli-occupied Golan Heights from Syria. 

Israel has denied reports that its troops have moved beyond the buffer zone and close to Damascus. 

We spoke to Michael Milshtein from the Moshe Dayan Center for Middle Eastern and African Studies at Tel-Aviv University and journalist Ehud Yaari on Israel's attacks.

DW Correspondent Tania  KrĂ¤mer also joined us from the occupied Golan Heights, where Israeli ground forces have entered Syria.

#syria #israel #middleeast 

00:00 Israel's attacks in Syria
03:00 Michael Milshtein discusses Israel's targets
09:25 Ehud Yaari updates on Israeli operations inside Syria
14:29 DW's Tania  KrĂ¤mer gives the latest from the Golan Heights

items.title: Netanyahu rails against charges as he takes stand at own corruption trial | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has testified at his own trial for corruption for the first time in Tel Aviv. 

Netanyahu railed at the charges against him, describing the allegations as an "ocean of absurdity."

He is accused of fraud, embezzlement, and bribery in three separate cases spanning back years.

Netanyahu is the first sitting Israeli prime minister to be charged with a crime. 

Judges have ordered him to testify three times a week in the coming weeks, with the proceedings not expected to wrap up for more than a year.

#netanyahu #israel #netanyahutrial

items.title: Can ATACMS help Ukraine hang on to Russian territory as it loses ground in Kursk? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
Nearly four months since Ukrainian troops moved into Russia's Kursk region, commanders say they are losing ground. 

Ukraine holds less than two-thirds of the territory it captured, while Russia is making more gains though at a heavy cost. 

DW's Rebecca Ritters reported from close to the frontline, where Ukrainian soldiers hope more heavy weapons will make a difference.  

We spoke to military expert Marina Miron from King's College London for more on Ukraine's struggles on the battlefield.

00:00 Ukraine losing ground in Russia's Kursk
03:31 Marina Miron on difficulties Ukraine faces

#ukraine #russia #kursk

items.title: Germany starts Syrian migration debate after Assad's fall | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
The overthrow of Bashar al-Assad has raised questions about the status of the many Syrians who fled to Europe. Some governments are already planning to deport Syrians or suspend their asylum claims. Syrians in Germany have been celebrating the end of the Assad regime, but they also face uncertainty. Authorities have stopped processing asylum requests from Syrians until there is more clarity on the political situation in their homeland. And some politicians are already suggesting Syrians should return home.

00:00 Syrians in Europe celebrate Assad's fall
01:05 Ehab Badwi, Syrian Youth Assembly founder
07:29 Johann Wadephul (CDU), Member of the German Bundestag

items.title: A new Islamist threat? Who are Syria's fragmented rebel groups and what's their influence | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
While Syrian rebel leader Al-Jolani's islamist HTS group is currently the face of the revolution in Syria, the opposition to the ousted Assad regime has been fragmented. Some fear that its most radical force, the Islamic State terrorist group, could use the current fragility in Syria to rebuild its capabilities. Turkey has played a significant and multi-faceted role in recent events in Syria, having backed groups like the Syrian National Army, which also opposed the Assad regime. Ankara has worked hard to prevent the establishment of a Kurdish-controlled autonomous regioon near its border.

00:00 New Islamist threat?
00:55 Behnam Ben Taleblu, Foundation of Defense of Democracies
06:30 AslÄ± AydÄ±ntaĹźbaĹź, Brookings Institution

items.title: Druze communities in the Golan Heights celebrate Assad's fall | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
More than 20,000 Druze still predominantly live in four villages in the Golan Heights. The Druze are an ethno-religious Arab sect and most of them identify as Syrian. Most Druze living in the Golan Heights also rejected an offer of Israeli citizenship when Israel seized the region in 1967. Those who refused were given Israeli residency cards but are not considered Israeli citizens.
